title: Charred Moroccan Colby Jack Cheese Tagine
description: A delicious, crispy Moroccan-inspired dish featuring Colby Jack cheese, perfect for a midnight craving. Serve with crusty bread or over couscous.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the mixture onto the inside of the tagine.
Place the shredded Colby Jack cheese in the tagine, leaving a small border around the edges.
Drizzle the water over the cheese, followed by the Manteo mixture.
Cover the tagine and bake for 10 minutes, or until the cheese starts to melt.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Use a spatula to carefully create a crispy, burnt crust on the cheese by moving it back and forth in a zigzag pattern.
Return the tagine to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and crispy.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired, and serve immediately.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Place the clay tagine over medium heat and add the shredded cheese.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Use a spatula to create a crispy, burnt crust on the cheese by moving it back and forth in a zigzag pattern.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
